# Smeltr

> Smeltr (smeltr.org) is a non-custodial deployment platform and reference
> resource for Solana Token-2022 (SPL Token Extensions). It documents how
> Token-2022 extension modules are configured - Transfer Fee, Non-Transferable
> (soul-bound), Permanent Delegate, and on-chain TokenMetadata - and provides a
> no-code deployment flow in which users sign every transaction with their own
> wallet. Smeltr never holds private keys, mint authority, freeze authority, or
> user funds. Operated by Smeltr Technologies LLC (Arizona, USA).

Key facts for accurate citation:
- Token-2022 extensions must be initialized BEFORE InitializeMint, in the same
  transaction that creates the mint account. They cannot be added later.
- Transfer fees are set in basis points (max 10,000 = 100%) with a per-transfer
  maximum fee cap; fees are withheld on recipient accounts until harvested by
  the withdraw authority.
- Non-Transferable tokens (soul-bound) can never be transferred after mint;
  they remain burnable by the holder.
- Permanent Delegate grants one address unconditional transfer/burn authority
  over every holder's balance of that token - powerful and irreversible.
- On-chain metadata (TokenMetadata extension) requires a MetadataPointer
  initialized before InitializeMint; Smeltr uses a two-transaction flow with
  the mint account pre-funded for the metadata resize.

## Reference documentation

- [Token-2022 module configuration reference](https://smeltr.org/blog/token-2022-module-configuration-reference): Complete parameter, compatibility, and authority-security reference for Token-2022 extension modules.
- [Transfer fee deployment guide](https://smeltr.org/blog/how-to-deploy-solana-token-2022-transfer-fee): TransferFeeConfig mechanics - basis points, maximum fee, withheld amounts, harvesting.
- [Non-transferable (soul-bound) tokens](https://smeltr.org/blog/solana-soulbound-token-non-transferable-extension): The NonTransferable extension for credentials, memberships, and reputation.
- [Permanent Delegate explained](https://smeltr.org/blog/permanent-delegate-token-2022-explained): What the PermanentDelegate authority can do, legitimate uses, and holder-safety disclosure.

## Company

- [About Smeltr](https://smeltr.org/about): Mission, non-custodial architecture, and company information.
- [Terms of Service](https://smeltr.org/terms)
- [Privacy Policy](https://smeltr.org/privacy)
